name: Busby Berkeley
description: American film director and musical choreographer (1895-1976)
abstract: Berkeley William Enos (November 29, 1895 – March 14, 1976), known professionally as Busby Berkeley, was an American film director and musical choreographer, best known for his collaboration with Warner Brothers in the early to mid-1930s. Berkeley devised elaborate musical production numbers that often involved complex geometric patterns.

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: I think they called him Busby Berkeley, and he was not a dancer, but he had Mr. Buddy Bradley doing the choreographies for those dance numbers in the movies

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I think Busby Berkeley, as I recall, he was the producer... It was Busby Berkeley. It wasn't Vincent Minnelli, but I did work with him, too.
